rows.title: Philippines NPC issues Advisory 2026-01 on data scraping of publicly available personal data
rows.summary: The Philippine National Privacy Commission issued Advisory No. 2026-01 on 13 April 2026, providing guidelines on the data scraping of publicly available personal data. The Advisory addresses the scope of the Data Privacy Act in relation to data scraping practices and conditions for lawful processing.

rows.title: Philippines Supreme Court issues A.M. 25-11-28-SC establishing AI governance framework for the judiciary
rows.summary: Philippines Supreme Court En Banc issued A.M. No. 25-11-28-SC on 18 February 2026, establishing a risk-based governance framework for AI in the judiciary. Risk tiers (prohibited / high-risk / limited-risk / minimal-risk), prior authorization, auditability, monitoring. AI must not serve as the sole, primary, or determinative basis of any adjudicatory outcome.

rows.title: Philippines NPC opens applications for Accountability Agent under APEC and Global CBPR Systems
rows.summary: The Philippine National Privacy Commission opened applications for recognition as an Accountability Agent under the APEC Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) System and the Global CBPR System. Initial application period: 27 January – 27 February 2026 (extended to 20 March 2026); nominations period extended to 16 April 2026. Separate applications are required for APEC and Global CBPR — recognition under one does not constitute recognition under the other. Accountability Agents certify whether participating organisations meet CBPR requirements.
